name: Bed Alcove
paraphrase: Beds belong in alcoves rather than floating in the middle of a generic bedroom: a low ceiling, partial walls, or a deep recess around the bed gives sleepers the enclosure and intimacy they instinctively want. Alexander argues that turning the whole bedroom into one big bed-island wastes space and feels exposed, while a built-in alcove frees the rest of the room for other uses and makes the bed itself a small, sheltered world.
